checks.name: Tensor product decomposition
checks.description: 5 ⊗ 5 ⊗ 5 decomposes into irreps with total dimension 125

checks.name: Kernel filter idempotence
checks.description: K² eigenvalues at kernel are zero (projector property)

checks.name: Casimir eigenvalue clustering
checks.description: All states segregate by j quantum number via Casimir operator

mathematical_framework.hilbert_space: V = V_2 ⊗ V_2 ⊗ V_2 where V_2 is Spin-2 irrep
mathematical_framework.casimir_operator: C = J_x² + J_y² + J_z² with eigenvalues λ = j(j+1)
mathematical_framework.kernel_filter: K = (C - 6I)(C - 30I) targets j=2 (λ=6) and j=5 (λ=30) sectors
mathematical_framework.invariant_kernel: E_47 = ker(K) is the 47-dimensional invariant subspace

reproducibility.expected_output: All VALID checks in console output
reproducibility.floating_point_notes: Eigenvalue rounding to 5 decimals removes numerical artifacts from diagonalization

notes: QuTiP's eigenenergies() method performs full diagonalization of Hermitian operators.
Kernel dimension extracted by counting eigenvalues == 0.0 after rounding.
Spectral gap is smallest positive eigenvalue of K², indicating decay rate of transient noise.
All computations are deterministic (no Monte Carlo sampling).
This validation forms the quantum foundation of the E47 paradigm.
